rag_rules: Use only identifiers that are visible in the owning component or subprogram scope.
When writing dependency contracts, relate visible outputs to visible inputs or parameters rather than internal paths.
Only add range assumptions or guarantees when concrete bounds, constants, or visible relations are provided.
generic_rules: Use model facts as the source of legal feature names, directions, and data types.
Treat visible subprogram in/out parameters as legal AGREE identifiers in their owning scope.
Keep every annex statement consistent with the scope of the component that owns the annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Valid statement forms include assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign; terminate each with a semicolon.
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool, and make helper declaration types match the expression type.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-only paths unless they are visible in the owning annex scope.
Do not invent constants, thresholds, or relations that are not supported by the requirement or model facts.
